title: Challenge Lab : Setting up the VPC environment, launching EC2 instances, and testing reachability using the AWS Reachability Analyzer

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.

On the AWS sign-in page, Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12-digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab. Now copy your Username and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12-digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your Username and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed in to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Sign in to AWS Portal

XYZ Company wants to test internal connectivity between resources within a custom Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) environment. As part of this, you are tasked with creating a secure VPC with both public and private subnets, launching EC2 instances in each, and verifying communication between them using AWS Reachability Analyzer. This setup helps ensure proper network configuration and access control between public-facing and internal resources.
Create VPC Name: reachability-test-VPC CIDR block: 10.0.0.0/16
Name: reachability-test-VPC
CIDR block: 10.0.0.0/16
Create Subnets Public Subnet: 10.0.1.0/24 Private Subnet: 10.0.2.0/24
Public Subnet: 10.0.1.0/24
Private Subnet: 10.0.2.0/24

Configure Internet Access Create a Route Table Name: MY-RT Create and attach an Internet Gateway Name: My-Internet-Gateway Add route: 0.0.0.0/0 ? target: Internet Gateway Associate MY-RT with the public subnet
Create a Route Table Name: MY-RT
Name: MY-RT
Create and attach an Internet Gateway Name: My-Internet-Gateway
Name: My-Internet-Gateway
Add route: 0.0.0.0/0 ? target: Internet Gateway
Associate MY-RT with the public subnet

Launch EC2 Instances Instance 1: Public Subnet Name: MyEC2Server1 AMI: Amazon Linux 2023 kernel-6.1 Type: t2.micro Key Pair: WhizKey (.pem, RSA) Network: VPC: reachability-test-VPC Subnet: public subnet Auto-assign Public IP: Enabled Security Group: MyEC2Server_SG Inbound: SSH + HTTP from Anywhere Instance 2: Private Subnet Name: MyEC2Server2 Same config as above, except: Subnet: private subnet Security Group: PrivateEC2Server_SG Inbound: Allow All Traffic from MyEC2Server_SG
Instance 1: Public Subnet Name: MyEC2Server1 AMI: Amazon Linux 2023 kernel-6.1 Type: t2.micro Key Pair: WhizKey (.pem, RSA)
Name: MyEC2Server1
AMI: Amazon Linux 2023 kernel-6.1
Type: t2.micro
Key Pair: WhizKey (.pem, RSA)
Network: VPC: reachability-test-VPC Subnet: public subnet Auto-assign Public IP: Enabled
VPC: reachability-test-VPC
Subnet: public subnet
Auto-assign Public IP: Enabled

Security Group: MyEC2Server_SG Inbound: SSH + HTTP from Anywhere Instance 2: Private Subnet Name: MyEC2Server2 Same config as above, except: Subnet: private subnet Security Group: PrivateEC2Server_SG Inbound: Allow All Traffic from MyEC2Server_SG
Inbound: SSH + HTTP from Anywhere
Instance 2: Private Subnet
Name: MyEC2Server2
Same config as above, except:
Subnet: private subnet Security Group: PrivateEC2Server_SG Inbound: Allow All Traffic from MyEC2Server_SG
Security Group: PrivateEC2Server_SG Inbound: Allow All Traffic from MyEC2Server_SG
Inbound: Allow All Traffic from MyEC2Server_SG
Run Reachability Test Open Reachability Analyzer Create analysis: Name: ec2-to-ec2-reachability Source: MyEC2Server1 Destination: MyEC2Server2 Analyze path
Open Reachability Analyzer
Create analysis: Name: ec2-to-ec2-reachability Source: MyEC2Server1 Destination: MyEC2Server2
Name: ec2-to-ec2-reachability
Source: MyEC2Server1
Destination: MyEC2Server2
Analyze path
Review Results Check if the result is Reachable If Unreachable, review diagnostics for cause
Check if the result is Reachable
If Unreachable, review diagnostics for cause
Click on the Validate to complete the challenge.
Sign out of the AWS Account.
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you have completed the steps, click on End Lab from the lab console.
